title: Showing multiple boards on a master board
excerpt:
Hi, 
I manage a team of designers, each has their own personal work board. 
I’ve been asked for all their work to appear on one master ‘team’ board. 
Is this possible?

posts.author: Jonedwards72 (Jon Edwards)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi,
I manage a team of designers, each has their own personal work board.
I’ve been asked for all their work to appear on one master ‘team’ board.
Is this possible?

posts.author: kaylak (Kayla K)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @Jonedwards72 ,

I think there are a couple solutions that might help you in this case.

  1. Creating a dashbaord with a table widget. The limitations of a dashboard would be dependent on the tier of plan that you have for Monday. For example, I believe Pro plan limits to 10 boards, so if there are more than 10 people, this would not work.
  2. Utilize the my work view and customize the view so you can see all of the team members. The major limitation of this is that it is not a view you can save and share with others.

posts.author: garfield-labs (Snir Alon)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi Jon, i think the app Same Item Multiple Boards is perfect for this use case

posts.author: BruceGosk (Bruce Gosk)
posts.cookedHtml: Check out Master Data - it was built for this. https://monday.com/marketplace/listing/10000552/master-data
